title: Replace Migrate Yes/No Toggle with Multi-Status Dropdown
story: As an end user, I want field-level migration status options beyond yes/no, so that I can track disposition and progress for each field.
description:
Replace the binary yes/no "Migrate" toggle on the migration field mapping page with a multi-status dropdown that combines disposition (skip) and progress tracking into a single field.

### New Status Options
- **Not Reviewed** (default, replaces NULL)
- **No Migration** (signals the field will be ignored)
- **In Progress**
- **Blocked**
- **QA**
- **Business Review**
- **Completed**

### Requirements
- Status dropdown is inline-editable directly on the page (no modal/edit window required)
- Color coding matches the existing scorecard status colors
- Applies to both migration mapping files
- Default value for unmapped fields is "Not Reviewed" instead of "Not Started"
acceptance_criteria: Migrate yes/no column is replaced with a multi-status dropdown
Dropdown includes: Not Reviewed, No Migration, In Progress, Blocked, QA, Business Review, Completed
Status is inline-editable without opening an edit modal
Default status for new/unmapped fields is Not Reviewed
ux_changes: Migrate column changes from a yes/no toggle to an inline-editable status dropdown with color-coded options matching the scorecard.
middleware_changes: Backend logic updated to handle the new status values instead of boolean migrate flag.
data_model_changes: Migration status column changes from boolean to an enum/string field supporting the seven status values.
implementation_notes: The status options mirror the scorecard component's existing status set for consistency.
comment_summary: Developer initially misunderstood the requirement as renaming yes/no labels. After clarification that the full scorecard status set was needed (Not Reviewed, No Migration, In Progress, Blocked, QA, Business Review, Completed), the feature was implemented and submitted for review.
